Start by defining two FROM statements — the first for building (AS builder) and the second for the final production image. Each FROM starts a fresh filesystem.
Leverage Docker layer caching by copying package*.json and running npm ci BEFORE copying the rest of the source code. This way, dependencies are only reinstalled when package files change.
In the production stage, use COPY --from=builder to selectively copy only the compiled output (e.g., dist/) and production node_modules. Never copy source TypeScript files.
Add security best practices: set USER to a non-root user, set NODE_ENV=production, and use npm ci --only=production to exclude devDependencies from the final image.
